title: Popups and Ads
drDigitalIntro: Popups are windows that suddenly appear on websites — some are legitimate, some are scams. Safari blocks most popups automatically. Ads are how many websites make money — you'll see banner ads on news sites. Some ads are scams pretending to be download buttons or security warnings. Don't click on ads that look suspicious. If something says 'Click here to download!' but you're not on a download page, it's probably a fake ad.

items.label: Newsletter signup
items.preview: Want weekly recipe ideas? Enter your email to subscribe. You can unsubscribe anytime.

items.label: ⚠️ URGENT SECURITY ALERT
items.preview: YOUR MAC HAS 5 VIRUSES! CLICK NOW TO CLEAN IMMEDIATELY OR YOUR FILES WILL BE DELETED!

items.label: Cookie notice
items.preview: This website uses cookies to improve your experience. Accept or Decline.

playgroundTask.fakeExplanation: Fake virus warnings in the browser are one of the most common scams. Real security warnings NEVER come from a website popup — they come from your Mac's own software.

drDigitalSuccess: You caught the scam! Browser popups that claim you have viruses are always fake. Never click them.
drDigitalHint: All-caps urgency, exclamation marks, and 'your computer is infected' messages in a browser are 100% scams.
